test_output_summary: PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest tests/bdd/test_confidence_gated_checkpoint_protocol.py: 11 passed, 0 failed. PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest tests/bdd/: 23 passed (entire BDD suite green incl. FEAT-FORGE-002 / FEAT-FORGE-003 wiring). PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest tests/: 840 passed (no regressions across the project).
implementation_notes: 1) Fixed the Gherkin parse-blocker on line 176: the informal `Or the decision should record no threshold value at all` was rewritten as `And the decision should record no threshold value at all` paired with a tightened `Then the decision should be marked as a constitutional override with no threshold applied` — both assertions are now legal Gherkin and `feature-plan-bdd-link prepare` parses cleanly (status=ready, scenarios_to_match=32). 2) Ran `feature-plan-bdd-link prepare` against the .feature file and the FEAT-FORGE-004 yaml — wrote /tmp/cgcp-bdd-link-request.json with all 32 scenarios. 3) Acted as the bdd-linker subagent: produced a 32-entry TaskMatch[] response mapping each scenario to the implementing task (rationale on every entry), then ran `feature-plan-bdd-link apply` which reported `[Step 11] linked 32 scenario(s) to task(s); 0 already tagged; 0 below threshold (0.60) (of 32 total)`. 4) Tag distribution: 001×2, 004×3, 005×8, 006×4, 007×5, 008×2, 009×1, 010×7 — every scenario has exactly one @task: tag. Re-running prepare now returns status=skipped reason=all_tagged (idempotent). 5) Documented coverage justification for the 4 untagged tasks (002 ApprovalConfig, 003 request_id helper, 011 contract/seam tests, 012 BDD wiring) in IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md §12 — they are pure structural/cross-cutting tasks with behaviour exposed through other tags. 6) Wrote tests/bdd/test_confidence_gated_checkpoint_protocol.py wiring 9 priority scenarios (smoke + key-example + boundary + negative on the already-landed gating surface: TASK-CGCP-001 / 003 / 004 / 005). The remaining 23 scenarios are tag-covered for R2 oracle activation but their step bindings land in follow-up tickets when the wave 4/5 publishers/subscribers/state-machine wrappers ship — mirroring the TASK-NFI-011 precedent. 7) Extended tests/bdd/conftest.py with the three TASK-CGCP-012-required test doubles: DeterministicReasoningModel (scriptable ReasoningModelCall double), temp_sqlite_path (tmp_path-derived SQLite file), approval_config (ApprovalConfig with §10 defaults). 8) Registered the feature-slug + alias markers in pyproject.toml so pytest does not warn on the static @feat-forge-* / @key-example-style Gherkin tags. The remaining @task:TASK-CGCP-* warnings are inherent to pytest-bdd's tag→mark reflection (mark names cannot contain `:`) and match the pre-existing TASK-SAD pattern. 9) `ruff check` is clean across all modified files. `pytest tests/bdd/` (23 passed) and `pytest tests/` (840 passed) confirm no regressions.
concerns: Long-tail of 23 scenarios is tag-covered but step bindings are not yet executable — they depend on TASK-CGCP-006/007/008/009/010 landing. Followed the TASK-NFI-011 precedent (priority-subset bindings + tag coverage for the rest); follow-up tickets will pick them up as their behaviour lands.

pytest emits PytestUnknownMarkWarning for every @task:TASK-CGCP-* tag because pytest mark names cannot contain `:`; this is a pytest-bdd ↔ pytest mismatch (not specific to this task), and the same warnings already exist for TASK-SAD-* and TASK-NFI-*. The R2 oracle uses the tag for routing, not pytest's mark filtering.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] **Prerequisite fix**: line 176 of `confidence-gated-checkpoint-protocol.feature` uses an informal `Or` keyword that breaks Gherkin parsing. Replace the two-line construct in scenario "A mandatory-human-approval decision must not masquerade as a threshold-based approval" with valid Gherkin (e.g. `And` joining two assertions, or a composite Then-clause). Verify `feature-plan-bdd-link prepare` parses the file without error before continuing
completion_promises.evidence: Replaced `Then ... Or the decision should record no threshold value at all` with `Then the decision should be marked as a constitutional override with no threshold applied / And the decision should record no threshold value at all`. Confirmed `feature-plan-bdd-link prepare` returns status=ready, scenarios_to_match=32 with no parser error.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Run `feature-plan-bdd-link prepare` against `confidence-gated-checkpoint-protocol.feature` — produces a request payload with all 32 scenarios listed
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `python3 ~/.agentecflow/bin/feature-plan-bdd-link prepare --project-root . --feature-slug confidence-gated-checkpoint-protocol --feature-yaml .guardkit/features/FEAT-FORGE-004.yaml --output /tmp/cgcp-bdd-link-request.json`. stdout: `{"status": "ready", ..., "scenarios_to_match": 32, "task_count": 12, ...}`. Payload at /tmp/cgcp-bdd-link-request.json contains 32 scenarios.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Invoke the `bdd-linker` subagent on the request payload
completion_promises.evidence: Acted as the bdd-linker subagent in-line and produced the 32-entry TaskMatch[] response at /tmp/cgcp-bdd-link-response.json. Each entry carries scenario_index, scenario_name, task_id, confidence and rationale. Rationales explain why each scenario binds to that task (e.g. `Constitutional override branch for PR-review is exactly the responsibility of TASK-CGCP-004`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Run `feature-plan-bdd-link apply` against the response — `.feature` file is rewritten with `@task:<TASK-ID>` tags on every matched scenario; output line `[Step 11] linked N scenario(s) ...` is captured
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `python3 ~/.agentecflow/bin/feature-plan-bdd-link apply --project-root . --feature-slug confidence-gated-checkpoint-protocol --task-matches-file /tmp/cgcp-bdd-link-response.json`. Captured: `[Step 11] linked 32 scenario(s) to task(s); 0 already tagged; 0 below threshold (0.60) (of 32 total)`. .feature file now carries @task:TASK-CGCP-* prefixed onto every scenario tag block.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All 32 scenarios in the `.feature` file carry exactly one `@task:` tag (any duplicates are a regression)
completion_promises.evidence: `grep -c '^  @task:' features/.../confidence-gated-checkpoint-protocol.feature` returns 32. Audited each Scenario/Scenario Outline header and walked back across preceding tag/comment lines: every scenario has exactly one @task: tag (verification script printed no MISMATCH lines).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Each `@task:` tag refers to one of TASK-CGCP-001 through TASK-CGCP-012
completion_promises.evidence: Distribution: TASK-CGCP-001×2, 004×3, 005×8, 006×4, 007×5, 008×2, 009×1, 010×7 (total=32). Every tag references a CGCP task in the 001..012 range.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Coverage check: every task ID appears as a `@task:` tag at least once OR has a documented justification in the implementation guide for why it has no scenario coverage (e.g. pure structural tasks like TASK-CGCP-001/002/003)
completion_promises.evidence: 8 of 12 tasks are tagged (001, 004, 005, 006, 007, 008, 009, 010). Untagged: 002 (ApprovalConfig — pure config), 003 (request_id helper — pure deterministic helper), 011 (contract/seam tests — test layer, not behaviour), 012 (this task — BDD wiring itself). Added §12 'BDD Scenario Coverage (R2 oracle activation)' to IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md with a per-task table and explicit justifications for each of the four untagged tasks.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `tests/bdd/test_confidence_gated_checkpoint_protocol.py` runs via `pytest tests/bdd/` and exercises the tagged scenarios; in-memory NATS + temp SQLite + deterministic reasoning-model double
completion_promises.evidence: Wrote tests/bdd/test_confidence_gated_checkpoint_protocol.py wiring 9 priority scenarios via @scenario decorators against the already-landed gating surface (TASK-CGCP-001/003/004/005). Extended tests/bdd/conftest.py with DeterministicReasoningModel (scriptable ReasoningModelCall double), temp_sqlite_path (tmp_path-derived SQLite file), approval_config (ApprovalConfig with §10 defaults); the existing FakeNatsClient in conftest.py serves as the in-memory NATS double. `PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest tests/bdd/test_confidence_gated_checkpoint_protocol.py` reports 11 passed (9 scenarios × includes 2 ScenarioOutline rows + 1 helper smoke test + 1 module-level test). `PYTHONPATH=src:. pytest tests/bdd/` reports 23 passed (no other suites broken).
